source_paper_title: Efficient and Near-Optimal Online Portfolio Selection
context:
Page 3, Section 1 (Introduction)

Meanwhile, no algorithm except Universal Portfolios is known to admit a regret bound matching (5) up to a constant factor. In other words, the following problem stands:
Propose a computationally feasible and near-optimal online portfolio selection algorithm.
title: Fast and regret-optimal online portfolio selection with optimal constants
latex:
Given an online portfolio selection game with \(d\ge 2\) assets and horizon \(T\ge 1\), in each round \(t\in\{1,\dots,T\}\) the learner chooses \(w_t\in\Delta_d:=\{w\in\mathbb{R}^d_+:\sum_{i=1}^d w[i]=1\}\), then the adversary reveals returns \(x_t\in\mathbb{R}^d_+\), and the learner suffers logarithmic loss \(\ell_t(w):=-\log(x_t^\top w)\). The regret against the best constant rebalanced portfolio (CRP) is
\[
R_T(w_{1:T}\mid x_{1:T}) := \sum_{t=1}^T \ell_t(w_t) - \min_{w\in\Delta_d}\sum_{t=1}^T \ell_t(w).
\]
An algorithm is called regret-optimal if it guarantees for all sequences \(x_{1:T}\in(\mathbb{R}^d_+)^T\) that
\[
R_T(w_{1:T}\mid x_{1:T}) \le C\,(d-1)\log(T+1) + c(d)
\]
for a universal constant \(C\) matching the optimal constant factor in the known worst-case lower bound (up to lower-order terms), while being computationally feasible (e.g. polynomial-time per round, ideally near \(\tilde O(d^2)\) or \(\tilde O(d^2T)\) per-round time without MCMC integration).

Problem: Design such a computationally feasible online portfolio selection algorithm achieving the optimal \(\Theta(d\log T)\) regret with the optimal constant factor (as in Universal Portfolios) and substantially improved per-round runtime compared to existing implementations of Universal Portfolios.
